title: Best Brick Cleaner of 2026: Formulas That Actually Remove Mold
excerpt: Choosing the wrong brick cleaner can waste time and harm plants. This guide matches eco-friendly or bleach-based formulas to your specific stain type and surface.
author: Editorial Team
metaDescription: Choosing the wrong brick cleaner can waste time and harm plants. This guide matches eco-friendly or bleach-based formulas to your specific stain type and surface.
intro:
Too many brick cleaner purchases end in disappointment. A product that promises to erase mold and black stains often fails on contact, while another cleans perfectly but leaves behind a bleach smell that burns your nose and bleaches your clothes. Without comparing formulas, it's a guessing game.

The market splits into two camps: fast-acting bleach-based cleaners that kill mildew almost instantly but require careful covering of plants, and eco-friendly peroxide formulas that are gentler but can falter on heavy, embedded stains. Choosing the right one depends on what's staining your brick and how much effort you're willing to put in.

A brick cleaner that matches your stain type will revive your patio in minutes, while the wrong one can cost you an afternoon and a ruined shirt.

products.title: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner Dirt,Mold & Mildew Cleaner & Stain Remover Concentrate | 1 Gallon
products.label: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Bleach-based concentrate that erases mold and mildew in seconds with zero scrubbing.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Wear old clothes and eye protection, and rinse any overspray from plants and soft surfaces within minutes to prevent bleaching.
pros: Mold and mildew stains lift away with a simple spray-and-rinse — no scrubbing needed. — 687 mentions, 86.6% positive on effectiveness; 170 mentions, 86.5% on no-scrub performance.
Restores brick, concrete, and siding to a nearly new appearance. — 88 mentions, 88.6% positive on appearance restoration.
Mixes and applies quickly with a garden sprayer or pressure washer. — 100 mentions, 98% positive on ease of use.
cons: The bleach odor can be overwhelming in still air, and splashes may lighten clothes and fabric. — 39 mentions, 77% negative on odor — widely reported bleach smell.
products.verdict: The fastest, most budget-friendly way to restore mold-stained brick and concrete — just gear up for the odor and rinse fabrics quickly.
products.review:
The bleach-based concentrate makes short work of black mold and mildew on brick, concrete, and siding. Mix equal parts water in a pump sprayer, apply, and let it dwell for half a minute; a rinse alone lifts stains that usually demand intense brushing. The formula restores deeply stained patios and walkways to a noticeably brighter state, often making aged brick look freshly laid. It handles the most common outdoor discoloration across fences, canvas awnings, and even plastic furniture without separate steps or tools.

This is the right pick for homeowners tackling large outdoor areas marred by mold and algae, and who prioritize speed and cost over a bleach-free formula. The strong chlorine odor during application calls for open-air use, protective eyewear, and old clothing — splashes can lighten fabric permanently. Rinsing overspray from nearby plants promptly keeps landscaping safe, but the smell makes it less suited for enclosed patios or entryways. Those who prefer a gentle, scent-free wash will find peroxide-based concentrates a better match, though they typically require longer dwell times and clean black mold less decisively.

💡 💡 Tip: Wear old clothes and eye protection, and rinse any overspray from plants and soft surfaces within minutes to prevent bleaching.

products.title: Savogran 10621 Trisodium Phosphate (TSP) 1LB (16oz)
products.label: Savogran TSP Heavy-Duty Cleaner
products.oneLiner: TSP powder that strips heavy grease and soot from brick before painting.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Store the powder in a sealed container to prevent moisture from hardening it into a brick; if it solidifies, smack it with a hammer and dissolve in hot water.
pros: Dissolves heavy grease, soot, and chalked paint faster than liquid cleaners. — 559 mentions, 88.7% positive
Excellent value for a deep-cleaning concentrate that lasts through multiple jobs. — 76 mentions, 88.2% positive
Works as a laundry and dishwasher booster for added household utility. — 70 mentions, 92.9% positive
cons: Powder can harden into a solid block if moisture enters the box, requiring chipping or hot water to dissolve. — 44 mentions, 50% negative on hardness
products.verdict: If your brick needs degreasing or paint prep, this powder outperforms spray cleaners at a lower cost — just keep it dry.
products.review:
Savogran TSP is a concentrated alkaline powder that cuts through soot, grease, and old chalked paint on brick without bleach fumes. DIY painters preparing brick walls or fireplaces for paint or stain get the deepest clean possible. The trade-off is mixing with water, wearing gloves, and thorough rinsing. The powder can also harden into a solid brick in humid storage, requiring chipping or hot water to dissolve. For exterior mold and mildew, the top pick's no-scrub bleach spray is faster, but for oil-based grime and pre-paint prep, TSP's alkaline attack is far more effective.

💡 💡 Tip: Store the powder in a sealed container to prevent moisture from hardening it into a brick; if it solidifies, smack it with a hammer and dissolve in hot water.

products.title: Simple Green Oxy Solve Total Outdoor Cleaner Concentrate, Pressure Washer & Manual Use, EPA Safer Choice Certified, Peroxide Cleaner for Wood, Concrete, Vinyl & More
products.label: Oxy Solve Total Outdoor Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Peroxide-powered concentrate for pressure washers, safe around plants and pets.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Let the solution sit on brick for 10-15 minutes before rinsing to improve mildew removal.
pros: Lifts dirt and light mildew from brick and mixed surfaces — 187 mentions, 70.6% positive
Bleach-free concentrate won't damage surrounding plants or discolor brick
Broad compatibility across wood, vinyl, brick, and concrete — 43 mentions, 86% positive
cons: May not fully remove heavy black mold or deeply set stains on brick — works best as a maintenance cleaner — 414 mentions, 35% negative
products.verdict: For brick patios and walkways that need regular cleanings without bleaching risk, Oxy Solve delivers a dirt-lifting maintenance wash — just know it can't restore heavily mold-stained brick on its own.
products.review:
Unlike fast-acting bleach formulas, Simple Green Oxy Solve uses peroxide to clean dirt and light mildew from brick, wood, and concrete without harming surrounding plants or leaving harsh residues. The concentrate mixes easily for pressure washing or manual spray application and covers up to 6,400 sq ft per gallon — practical for maintaining brick patios and walkways.

Its limit becomes apparent on heavily embedded black mold or deeply stained brick. Peroxide can struggle, sometimes leaving behind a film or requiring extra dwell time and scrubbing. This makes it a better fit for routine maintenance rather than full restoration. Eco-conscious homeowners who prioritize pet safety and plant health over maximum stain-fighting aggressiveness will find it a solid, gentle cleaner.

💡 💡 Tip: Let the solution sit on brick for 10-15 minutes before rinsing to improve mildew removal.

products.title: Krud Kutter Original Cleaner Degreaser Spray, 32 oz, Heavy Duty Multi Surface Cleaner for Grease, Oil and Tough Stains, Ready to Use for Kitchen, Garage, Floors and Outdoor Surfaces
products.label: Krud Kutter Original Degreaser
products.badge: Best Degreaser for Oily Brick Stains
products.oneLiner: Spray-on degreaser that melts engine oil and grill grease, no harsh fumes.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Keep a heavy-duty spray head handy—the included one can sputter and die.
pros: Easily breaks down petroleum-based grease and oil on brick and concrete without harsh scrubbing. — 660 mentions, 85.6% positive
No strong chemical odor—workable in attached garages and enclosed patios. — 103 mentions, 78.6% positive
Ready-to-use spray eliminates mixing; just spray, wait, and wipe. — 65 mentions, 90.7% positive
cons: The factory sprayer may stop working after repeated use; a durable replacement spray head keeps the bottle functional. — 31 mentions, 39% negative
products.verdict: For oil-stained brick driveways and garage spots, this budget-friendly degreaser handles the mess without fume complaints. Mold and whole-surface cleaning call for the bleach-based top pick.
products.review:
This degreaser tackles oil spots and greasy residue that power washers miss. The spray-on formula breaks down petroleum-based grime, then wipes away without scrubbing—effective on brick driveways, garage floors, and outdoor kitchen tiles. Without harsh fumes, it's usable in enclosed garages where bleach smells would linger.

It's ideal for spot-cleaning oil drips, grill splatter, and greasy tools on brick. The ready-to-use bottle works for small stains, but the included sprayer may become unreliable; a heavy-duty replacement head makes the experience frustration-free. For mold, mildew, or large-area driveway coverage, the bleach-based top pick is the better tool.

💡 💡 Tip: Keep a heavy-duty spray head handy—the included one can sputter and die.

products.title: Black Diamond Stoneworks MARBLE & TILE FLOOR CLEANER. Great for Ceramic, Porcelain, Granite, Natural Stone, Vinyl and Brick. No-rinse Concentrate.(1-Gallon)
products.label: Black Diamond Stoneworks MARBLE & TILE FLOOR CLEANER
pros: Leaves interior brick and stone floors clean and shiny without residue — 497 mentions, 82.1% positive on cleaning performance; 200 mentions, 80.5% positive on shine
Safe pH-neutral formula won't etch or dull delicate stone and brick surfaces — 176 mentions, 93.8% positive on quality
cons: May leave streaks or a film on some surfaces if dilution isn't precise or water is hard — 180 mentions, 36% negative on streak-free performance
products.verdict: For interior brick and stone floors that need a gentle, glossy maintenance wash without rinsing, this delivers. Skip it for any outdoor brick that faces mold, mildew, or deep stains.
products.review: This pH-neutral no-rinse concentrate keeps interior brick and stone floors gleaming with a light lemon scent and won't dull delicate surfaces. But it's built exclusively for indoor maintenance — it lacks the mold-killing agents and heavy stain removal needed for exterior brick. For that, outdoor-specific cleaners cost less and do more.

products.title: Oxy Solve House and Siding Pressure Washer Cleaner - Removes Stains from Mold & Mildew on Vinyl, Aluminum, Wood, Brick, Stucco - Concentrate 1 Gal.
products.label: Oxy Solve House & Siding Cleaner
pros: Leaves brick and siding looking noticeably fresher after a wash. — 24 mentions, 91.7% positive on appearance
Non-toxic, peroxide-based formula won't damage plants or contaminate waterways. — 19 mentions, 78.9% positive on safety
cons: Mold and mildew removal is inconsistent — expect lighter cleaning rather than heavy stain lifting. — 284 mentions, 41.2% negative
products.verdict: Only makes sense for eco-sensitive locations where bleach is banned and you're dealing with light dirt — not the answer for stubborn mold on brick.
products.review: Oxy Solve lifts light dirt and general grime from brick and siding while keeping nearby plants and waterways safe. When faced with stubborn mold or mildew, its cleaning power is noticeably weaker than bleach-based alternatives — results can be inconsistent. For homes near lakes or ponds where chemical runoff is restricted, it fills a narrow niche as a maintenance wash rather than a heavy-duty stain remover.

buyingGuide.heading: Mold & Mildew Removal
buyingGuide.body:
Bleach-based cleaners (sodium hypochlorite) oxidize mold spores on contact, making them ideal for severe black stains on shaded patios. Peroxide formulas are gentler but need longer dwell times and often require two or three passes to match bleach's speed.

For greasy soot or oil, a degreaser like TSP is needed; bleach won't cut through hydrocarbons. Match the cleaner's chemistry to your stain type to avoid wasted effort.

buyingGuide.heading: Surface Compatibility
buyingGuide.body: Outdoor brick is porous, and runoff can damage plants. Bleach and TSP rinsate can burn grass if not diluted and hosed off thoroughly. Peroxide and biodegradable cleaners leave no harmful residue, making them safer for flower beds and waterways.

buyingGuide.heading: Ease of Application
buyingGuide.body: Concentrates mix with water in a sprayer or pressure washer, covering large areas efficiently. Ready-to-use sprays skip measuring but are uneconomical beyond a few square feet. Powder concentrates like TSP must be fully dissolved and rinsed to avoid etching, adding steps but delivering deep clean.

buyingGuide.heading: Coverage Area
buyingGuide.body: Concentrate labels often quote theoretical square feet under ideal dilution, but heavily soiled brick absorbs more solution, reducing real coverage. Check the dilution ratio—a 1:10 mix stretches much farther than a 1:1 mix. For patios over 500 sq ft, a gallon of strong concentrate is the most cost-effective.

buyingGuide.heading: Odor & Chemical Safety
buyingGuide.body: Bleach releases unpleasant fumes and can bleach clothing on contact; always cover plants and wear old clothes. Peroxide cleaners have a mild scent and won't ruin fabric. With any chemical, use gloves and eye protection against splashes.

faq.question: Why didn't my brick cleaner remove the black stains even after power washing?
faq.answer: Black stains on shaded brick are usually embedded mold or algae colonies that bleach immediately kills, but peroxide formulas need more contact time. If you used a peroxide cleaner, let it dwell for 15–30 minutes before rinsing and repeat if needed. For persistent black stains, switch to a bleach-based concentrate or pre-treat with TSP for paint prep.

faq.question: Can I use a bleach-based outdoor cleaner around my flower beds without killing them?
faq.answer: Yes, if you wet the plants first, cover them with plastic sheeting during application, and rinse the brick and surrounding soil thoroughly afterward. Bleach rinsate can be neutralized with plenty of water. Never let bleach puddles sit near roots.

faq.question: How do I dissolve solid TSP powder that turned into a brick?
faq.answer: Break the brick into chunks with a hammer, then add the pieces to a bucket of hot water while stirring. Hot water speeds dissolution. Avoid using boiling water in a sealed container due to pressure. The chunks will dissolve in minutes with agitation.

faq.question: Is Oxy Solve safe to use on brick near a lake or pond?
faq.answer: Oxy Solve's peroxide chemistry breaks down into water and oxygen, so it's a good choice where chemical runoff is a concern. Still, avoid direct discharge into the water by applying on a dry day and allowing plenty of rinse water to soak into the ground, rather than running off into the lake.
